content: In controlled studies, creatine supplementation significantly improved memory tasks in vegetarians and vegans, including working memory and number recall.
Creatine supplementation increased brain creatine levels, leading to better cognitive performance in vegetarians compared to non-supplemented individuals.
The mechanism of creatine supplementation involves facilitating the recycling of ATP in brain tissue, thereby increasing energy availability and cognitive function.
In vitro studies show that creatine elevates phosphocreatine and ATP levels, enhancing oxidative phosphorylation and overall brain energy.
For optimal cognitive benefits, a minimum daily dose of 5 grams of creatine is recommended, equivalent to consuming about 2.2 pounds of meat daily.
Higher doses of creatine, such as 10 or 20 grams per day, have been found to be beneficial, especially in a stressful environment where sleep quality may be compromised.
